title: "No route to host" while trying to curl to the container connected to multiple networks using netavark
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

When a container is publishing ports with <host_ip>:port, and is connected to multiple (non-internal) bridge network, using the network backend netavark, trying to curl the container with <host_ip>:port failed with "No route to host"

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create two bridge networks
podman network create --driver=bridge --subnet=192.168.230.0/24 network_main
podman network create --driver=bridge --subnet=192.168.228.0/24 network_sub

2. Create two containers
podman run -d  --network=network_main -p 10.85.41.219:9000:8080 --name nginx docker.io/bitnami/nginx:latest
podman run -d  --network=network_main -p 10.85.41.219:9090:8080 --name nginx123 docker.io/bitnami/nginx:latest

After running these commands, from the host
curl 10.85.41.219:9000
curl 10.85.41.219:9090
both are working.

3. Connect network_sub to container nginx
podman network connect network_sub nginx

**Describe the results you received:**
After running the above commands, while firing curl command from host:
curl 10.85.41.219:9000 shows "No route to host", which is container nginx, connected to network_main and network_sub
curl 10.85.41.219:9090 still works, which is container nginx123, only connects to network_main

**Describe the results you expected:**
curl 10.85.41.219:9000 should work as well

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Firewalld is disabled on the host

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.0
API Version:  4.0.0
Go Version:   go1.17.7

Built:      Mon Feb 21 13:35:55 2022
OS/Arch:    linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes, we also tried the Podman 4.0.2, the behavior is the same

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
I cannot reproduce with the latest version, what is your netavark version. `/usr/libexec/podman/netavark version` should show it.

Can you also provide the `iptables -nvL -t nat` output?

netavark version:
```
{
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "commit": "78e556ee22452bf70b1a0daffb0a98cd124e8354",
  "build_time": "2022-02-23T21:10:35.218058613+00:00",
  "target": "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"
}
```

Output from ```iptables -nvL -t nat```

A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This seems to be a RHEL only issue closing this in favour of https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2075373

@Luap99 do you remember what this bug was about? 

> You are not authorized to access bug #2075373. To see this bug, you must first [log in to an account](https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2075373&GoAheadAndLogIn=1) with the appropriate permissions.
